- I want to modify the right author portal.
- To be very functional for every single feature and every single element.
- To drive and get data from the database.
- To store data from the database.
- To use cloudinary for image upload.
- To store images and videos uploaded.
- To be displayed as news.
- To enhance every single item.
- To improve on the portal on the writer's article part, the place where you write the article, for it to be more functional.
- For functionalities to be well-placed.
- To improve on the UI.
- To give a better experience to the writers.

**Developing the Writer Portal**

Right now, I'm focusing on making the Writer Portal in KPH a lot more dynamic. I want it to pull data directly from Supabase, so it's always up-to-date. I'm also planning to integrate Cloudinary for easy media uploads. This will make the portal a real powerhouse for our writers. Finally, I'm thinking a fresh UI will make it more pleasant to use.


**Optimizing the Dashboard**

I'm currently focused on the dashboard's evolution.  I'm integrating data fetching and storage with Supabase. Cloudinary is in place for media uploads, with URLs recorded in the database.  The editorial studio is getting a rich text editor and better metadata management. I'm building out the `AdminService` and `UploadService` to manage articles and media. The dashboard is going to include real stats like views and likes.


**Designing the Editor & Dashboard**

I've been honing in on the editor and dashboard design. The editorial interface will offer a distraction-free mode, integrated media library with recent uploads, and support for categories, tags, and SEO metadata. I'm also adding video support via Cloudinary and an auto-save indicator. I'm working on the data structure to support stats, even if I simulate the numbers initially. On the dashboard, a left sidebar for easy navigation and a main content area with cards for stats and searchable stories list are planned. I'm making sure it's all easy to use.


**Adjusting Data Handling**

I'm currently dealing with the Supabase schema discrepancies I noted earlier, deciding to either embrace a flexible JSON approach or build a robust `articles` table within `AdminService` with more structure. I'm focusing on the `Dashboard.tsx` overhaul. I also plan to add video support to `uploadService.ts` and writer-specific methods to `adminService.ts`. I also added a "Media Library" component, video upload capability and improved article card details on the Dashboard.

To enhance the **Kwara Political Hangout Writer Portal**, I will overhaul the dashboard to provide a professional-grade editorial experience. The updates will focus on robust data persistence with Supabase, high-performance media handling via Cloudinary (supporting both images and video), and a sleek, distraction-free "Studio" for content creation.

### Specification of Updates
